title: Today's Markets Brief: Colombia stocks higher at close of trade; COLCAP up 3.57%
summary: A quick look at today's finance headlines

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at today's finance news. Let's dive right in. I've got a few items on my radar that I think are worth sharing.

First up, Colombia's stocks are having a great day. The COLCAP index is up 3.57% at the close of trade. According to Investing.com, this is the latest news on Colombia's stock market. It's always interesting to see emerging markets make moves like this. I mean, who doesn't love a good comeback story?

Next, Alphabet, the parent company of Google, is planning to raise a whopping $80 billion from stock sales. CNBC reports that this is all part of their plan to fund their AI buildout. A $10 billion investment from Berkshire Hathaway is also part of the deal. That's a serious vote of confidence in Alphabet's AI ambitions. I wonder what they're planning to build with all that cash.

And it's not just Alphabet making big moves. The AI space is heating up, with companies racing to build out their capabilities. It's like a tech arms race, but instead of missiles, it's servers and computing power.
Colombia stocks higher at close of trade; COLCAP up 3.57% is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
